habitat: Deciduous; semi-deciduous; and open evergreen woodland near water; ravines; plantations
story: The Brown Fish Owl (Ketupa zeylonensis) is an extraordinary bird that belongs to the Strigidae family. It is most commonly observed in Deciduous; semi-deciduous; and open evergreen woodland near water; ravines; plantations, where its behaviors contribute to the ecological dynamics of the area. Listed as Least Concern (LC) by the IUCN, this species' future depends on continued conservation action. The story of the Brown Fish Owl is one of adaptation and resilience, offering valuable lessons for conservationists. This poster aims to bring the Brown Fish Owl into focus, highlighting its beauty and the importance of its habitat.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
